rawIntent:
[Feature: Add a single GET /uptime route that returns JSON {uptimeSeconds: floor of proces — Phase 2: Phase 2: UptimeService implementation]

Create src/modules/uptime/uptime.service.ts. Implement UptimeService class that implements IUptimeService. The getUptime() method returns { uptimeSeconds: Math.floor(process.uptime()) }. This phase depends on src/modules/uptime/uptime.model.ts and src/modules/uptime/uptime.service.interface.ts from Phase 1 — read both before generating.

This phase depends on: Phase 1.

Phase architecture notes:
Pure computation with no side effects. process.uptime() is a built-in Node.js function.

successCriteria.description: src/modules/uptime/uptime.service.ts exists and exports UptimeService class
getUptime() returns { uptimeSeconds: number } where uptimeSeconds equals Math.floor(process.uptime())
No external dependencies, no database access, no side effects — pure synchronous computation

constraints: No database access — this is a pure computation service with no repository dependency
No side effects — getUptime() is a read-only synchronous method
No sensitive data in logs — process.uptime() returns a number of seconds, which is not PII or sensitive
Error handling — though the method is synchronous and cannot throw under normal operation, any unexpected errors must not propagate unhandled
Import only from canonical dependencies: IUptimeService from ./uptime.service.interface and UptimeStatus from ./uptime.model
